intro: Use this guide to select plug-in modules based on audience profile and team function. The core program (Modules 1-5) provides the foundation; plug-in modules customize the experience for specific audiences.

teams.title: HR Team Training

priorities.why: The defining HR challenge — coaching without authority
The adaptive challenge at the heart of HR leadership
Top CHRO priority for 2026; connects to psych safety
HR leads every restructuring; they need support too
Builds strategic credibility; aligns with AI module

priorities.module: A: Conflict as a Leadership Tool
priorities.why: HR navigates conflict daily; deepens Module 3
Deepens self-awareness for high-stress HR contexts
Relevant for senior HR leaders focused on succession

schedule.content: The HR Leader as 'Leader of Leaders'

teams.scheduleNote: Module 4 (Strategic Leader) and Module 5 (AI) are replaced by H1 and H5. Core frameworks from Module 4 are referenced within H2 if added as a follow-up session.

teams.title: Finance Team Training

priorities.why: The #1 skill gap for finance leaders; builds influence
Defines the modern finance leader's role
The daily reality of finance leadership in 2026
Extends Module 4 into finance's strategic sweet spot

priorities.module: F4: Building a Future-Ready Finance Team
priorities.why: Talent pipeline crisis in finance; aligns with AI module
Finance leaders partner across every function
Relevant for finance teams facing initiative overload

priorities.module: A: Conflict as a Leadership Tool
priorities.why: Useful for finance leaders navigating budget disputes

teams.scheduleNote: Module 4 (Strategic Leader) and Module 5 (AI) are replaced by F1 and F2. Ownership and decision-making themes from Module 4 are naturally embedded in F2 and F3.

teams.title: Engineering Team Training

priorities.why: The defining adaptive challenge for engineering managers
The #1 influence gap; connects technical work to business value

priorities.module: E3: Team Health, Burnout & Sustainable Pace
priorities.why: AI anxiety + 'do more with less' = burnout crisis
Extends Module 5 into engineering's specific AI reality

priorities.module: E4: Managing Technical Debt as Leadership
priorities.why: Reframes debt from technical issue to strategic conversation
Essential for senior EMs and directors

priorities.module: A: Conflict as a Leadership Tool
priorities.why: Useful for engineering-product tensions
Engineering teams drowning in competing demands

teams.scheduleNote: Module 4 (Strategic Leader) and Module 5 (AI) are replaced by E1 and E2. Ownership and decision-making themes are directly embedded in E1.
teams.altNote: Skips Module 3 coaching basics (senior leaders have likely had it) and Module 4/5 entirely, replacing them with strategically relevant engineering modules.

discussionIntro: Use this guide when meeting with each team to determine the final module selection.
questions: What are the top 3 leadership challenges your team faces right now?
Where does your team feel most confident as leaders? Where are the gaps?
What's the biggest change your team is navigating this year?
How would you describe the team's relationship with other functions?
What would make this training a success in your eyes? What should people walk away able to do?
decisionMatrixDesc: For each plug-in module, rate with the team: Relevance (1-5) and Urgency (1-5). Select the top 2-3 modules by total score. Ensure at least one 'Must-Have' is included.
